title: Field Widgets
text: Field Widgets Most widgets are field-level widgets that represent scalar data values. If no widget is explicitly selected, the default widget is used—it represents the field as a single line edit box. You can set widget properties for all field widgets in Properties Inspector. For field widgets in forms and dynamic server pages, you can also set them in the initialization file and in script. it is not possible to do this for field widgets in static server pages. In ProcScript, you can use: $properties to set the default widget properties $fieldproperties to set the widget properties of a specific field instance. $paintedfieldproperties to dynamically change the size and position of the widget. Note: Although the Widget Properties dialog of some widgets (such as list and spinner widgets) enables you to define the ValRep property, this is a field-level property and can be set with $valrep and $fieldvalrep . Field Widgets for Forms and Reports Form components support a wide range of widgets. Report components support only non-editable EditBox and Picture widgets. For more information, see Widgets for Forms and Reports . In character mode applications, widgets are not supported. In this case, Unifields are used. These are field representations that can handle all data types and work identically in both GUI (Windows) and character mode. For more information, see Unifields . Field Widgets for Dynamic Server Pages Field widgets for dynamic server pages are styled using properties that map to HTML attributes. They can be set in ProcScript, but it is more common to use JavaScript to reduce the need to get updates from the server. For more information, see Widgets for Dynamic Server Pages . For field widgets in dynamic server pages, you can also use the following JavaScript APIs to set widget properties in the browser: Table 1. JavaScript API Functions for Widget Properties Member Purpose clearProperties() Restores the initial widget properties of the object. clearProperty() Restores the initial value of the specified widget property. getProperties() Returns the widget properties of the object getProperty() Returns the value of a widget property of the object setProperties() Sets one or more widget properties for the object. setProperty() Sets the specified widget property for the object to a given value. Field Widgets for Static Server Pages These widgets are implemented as XHTML controls, and they do not support logical widgets. To set their properties, you can edit the server page layout. For more information, see Widgets for Static Server Pages and Customizing the XHTML Generated for USPs . Related Links Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages Widgets in dynamic server pages (DSPs) determine the appearance and behavior of fields, labels, occurrences, and entities displayed in the client browser. Widget Reference: Forms and Reports Widgets in Form components determine the appearance and behavior of fields, labels, occurrences, and entities in components running on Windows.
